demo.name: Cayenne Pepper
description: Large, crisp peppers used to season many a dish.
They make the flavor richer - and the bite hotter.
comment: Burns like a sea witch's poison.

demo.name: Banana
description: A sweet fruit. Found in the Coastal Jungle.

demo.name: Coconut
description: Found on the beach, and on palm trees along the shore in the Coastal Jungle.

demo.name: Rum Bottle
description: The finest seasoning for any pirate dish.
comment: We ran out of rum today.
The crew sobered up a bit... What a damned mess.
The scoundrels tried to hatch a plot...
- Edward Teach

demo.name: Coconut Milk with Bananas
description: A refreshing drink of coconut milk and ripe bananas.
Quenches thirst and lends strength for long fights beneath a scorching sun.
comment: A sip of pure paradise.

demo.name: Spicy "Chicken" with Sweet Potato
description: A hearty, spicy dish of meat, hot spices, and sweet potato.
Warms you up and restores your strength.
comment: One bite and your chest will blaze, your weariness burning to ash.
Your throat may suffer in the process.

demo.name: Bacon and Eggs
description: A hearty breakfast of fresh eggs and smoked bacon - the perfect start to a day in the Paradise Archipelago.
comment: A rare breakfast for a sailor, especially at sea.

demo.name: Boiled Crab
comment: Five minutes ago, it was scuttling along the shore...

demo.name: Spicy Breaded Kebab
comment: A true sailor eats meat off the fire, drinks rum straight from the bottle, and sleeps with a knife under his pillow.

demo.name: Skewered Meat
description: Meat with a hint of smoke.
comment: Perfect food - no fork, no knife, no manners required.
- "No-Hands" Flynn, carpenter

demo.name: Spicy Skewered Crab
description: Crisp, spiced, and hearty enough to satisfy your hunger and lift your mood.
comment: If your tongue isn't numb, the cook went easy on the spices.

demo.name: Hearty Egg Broth
description: A hearty stew cooked when the hold is full of supplies and the crew's been at sea for less than a week.
comment: If there's only one sweet potato left in the galley, we're nearing either a port or a mutiny.
- ship's cook "One-Eyed" Axel

demo.name: Dodo Broth
description: A delicate, crystal-clear broth made from dodo meat.
Served to the stick, the wounded... and sailors after a wild night at the tavern.
comment: The tempting smell of a boiling pot has brought more people back from the brink of death than any potion ever could.
- ship's cook "One-Eyed" Axel

demo.name: Coconut Soup
description: This thick coconut-milk soup brings hot shores to mind - even in a storm.
comment: Tastes like a paradise island that we will never reach.
- "Cooked Compass" Billy, navigator

demo.name: Coffee
description: Helps you last a few hours past sundown - and keeps you from putting a bullet in your head come morning.
comment: Love is like coffee: it can be sweet, it can be bitter, and in large doses, it can be lethal.
- Doctor Galen Skelton

EA0_10.name: Corn
description: It can be ground, roasted, boiled, pickled, baked, added to other dishes - and that's not even the full list.

EA0_10.name: Lime
comment: The best remedy for scurvy.

EA0_10.name: Tomato
description: They've been cultivated by ancient civilizations for centuries.
comment: Poisonous, not poisonous... If you can chew it, it'll do!
- Captain Thomas Richards, perpetually hungry

EA0_10.name: Burrito
description: A hearty wrap of tender corn tortilla, stuffed with chopped meat and spices.
comment: One bite, and a sea storm will feel like a light breeze.

EA0_10.name: Tortilla
description: Not a feast, but enough to quiet a growling stomach.
comment: Before you know it, you will devour a whole stack and ask for more.

EA0_10.name: Pastechi
description: Fried corn dough with a meat filling inside.
A traditional appetizer on the islands of Aruba, Bonaire and Curacao.
comment: On every ship, on every raid, there is bound to be some fool who swears pastechi with potatoes is better.
- Black Axel, cook

EA0_10.name: Sunmilk Tonic
description: A healing drink of coconut milk, aloe pulp, and crunchy roasted nuts.
comment: Nuts in a drink? Unthinkable blasphemy!
Reverend Father Ignacio

EA0_10.name: Refreshing Tomato Juice
description: Made from ripe tomatoes, this bold red drink is full of flavour.
comment: An acquired taste.
The color and smell aren't doing it any favors either.
Salt and rum might help.

EA0_10.name: Sweet Lime Juice
description: A sweet and sour drink made from freshly squeezed lime juice and cane sugar.
comment: Too sweet for the bosun, too sour for the cook, but just right for a cabin boy.

EA0_10.name: Seafood Platter
description: Fresh seafood, a sign of the ocean's generosity.
Keeps hunger away for a long time.
comment: A favorite dish among Tortuga's most enthusiastic charmers.

EA0_10.name: Fish and Tostones
description: Fish with golden, twice-fried banana slices.
A familiar dish in the Caribbean.
comment: On land, fish and bananas are just food.
At sea, it is a royal feast.

EA0_10.name: Seafood Skewer
comment: In case of failure, don't blame the fish.
- the fisherman's third rule

EA0_10.name: Chowder
description: A rich fish soup where you can toss in not just the fish, but whatever else came up with it.
comment: Any fish not sold during the day ends up in the evening soup.

EA0_10.name: Crab Soup with Diced Tomatoes
description: This thick soup, made from freshly caught crab and ripe tomatoes, is equally suited to a hungry sailor and a wealthy merchant alike.
comment: Its aroma gathers the whole crew around the cauldron faster than a cry of "Land ahoy!"

EA0_10.name: Banana Muffin
description: A sweet, moist muffin made from ripe bananas.
comment: It does not taste as good when made with rotten bananas.

EA0_10.name: Tangy Nut Roll
description: A rich pastry roll with a delicate nutty flavor.
comment: It leaves a tart aftertaste, a vivid reminder of former adventures.

EA0_10.name: Meat Salad
description: A perfect dish when you have a bit of this, a bit of that, and whatever else you can find.
The only trouble is chasing down the ingredients first.
comment: You eat your grass.
I'll take the meat.
